Release 1.1.1
  • macOS arm64
  • Linux x64
  • е…ЁйғЁеҸ‘иЎҢзүҲ

иҢғејҸpackages
зұ»еһӢstatic В· type-first
зӣ®ж ҮRust вҶ’ native
и®ёеҸҜиҜҒMIT

valor

Translation status: з®ҖдҪ“дёӯж–Ү reader-locale proof. Code fences render through the zh-Hans pipeline; prose is canonical Latin.

JSON-shaped dynamic value literal for open object-shaped data.

Syntax: valor

Category

conversion

Related

  • вҶҰ
  • valor
  • octeti
  • lista
  • tabula
  • genus
  • tensor

Examples

examples/corpus/conversio/valor-boxing.fab (canonical В· conversio)

T вҶҰ valor boxing for scalar, byte, collection, genus, and tensor carriers.

# =============================================================================
# valor вҖ” T вҶҰ valor boxing for scalar, byte, collection, genus, and tensor carriers.
# =============================================================================
#
# What this teaches:
#   вҖў Boxing into valor вҖ” `T вҶҰ valor` boxes any typed value (scalar, byte, collection, genus, tensor) into the canonical dynamic carrier.
#   вҖў Byte preservation вҖ” `octeti вҶҰ valor` composes as numeric byte values; it must not lossy-decode bytes as text.
#
# Common mistakes:
#   вҖў Expecting to extract a specific type from valor without a matching variant вҖ” valor requires the correct runtime variant or `вҮҘ` recovery.
#
# See also: вҶҰ, valor, octeti, lista, tabula, genus, tensor
# =============================================================================

# conversio вҖ” typed values to valor boxing
#
# WHY: `T вҶҰ valor` boxes typed values into the canonical dynamic carrier.
# `octeti` composes as a list of numeric byte values; it must not lossy-decode
# bytes as text.

genus Punctum {
    numerus x
    numerus y
}

incipit {
    fixum numerus n вҶҗ 42
    fixum valor scalar вҶҗ n вҶҰ valor
    nota scalar

    fixum octeti bytes вҶҗ |de ad|
    fixum valor boxedBytes вҶҗ bytes вҶҰ valor
    nota boxedBytes

    fixum lista<numerus> xs вҶҗ [1, 2]
    fixum valor boxedList вҶҗ xs вҶҰ valor
    nota boxedList

    fixum tabula<textus, numerus> scores вҶҗ { "alpha": 10 }
    fixum valor boxedMap вҶҗ scores вҶҰ valor
    nota boxedMap

    fixum Punctum pt вҶҗ Punctum { x = 3, y = 4 }
    fixum valor boxedGenus вҶҗ pt вҶҰ valor
    nota boxedGenus

    fixum tensor<numerus, [2]> grid вҶҗ [5, 6] вҶҰ tensor<numerus, [2]>
    fixum valor boxedTensor вҶҗ grid вҶҰ valor
    nota boxedTensor
}

Expected output:

42
[222, 173]
[1, 2]
{"alpha": 10}
{"x": 3, "y": 4}
[5, 6]

examples/corpus/conversio/valor-genus.fab (canonical В· conversio)

valor вҶҰ genus extraction with missing-field defaults and mandatory-field failure via вҮҘ recovery.

# =============================================================================
# valor вҖ” valor вҶҰ genus extraction with missing-field defaults and mandatory-field failure via вҮҘ recovery.
# =============================================================================
#
# What this teaches:
#   вҖў JSON-to-struct deserialization вҖ” `valor вҶҰ genus` deserializes JSON objects into typed genus records.
#   вҖў Field policies вҖ” missing `sponte` fields zero-initialize; missing mandatory fields fail at runtime (recoverable via `вҮҘ`); extra JSON keys are ignored.
#
# Common mistakes:
#   вҖў Omitting a mandatory field from the JSON and expecting a default value вҖ” only `sponte` or default-valued fields are optional; missing mandatory fields fail at runtime.
#
# See also: вҶҰ, вҮҘ
# =============================================================================

# conversio вҖ” valor to genus extraction
#
# WHY: `valor вҶҰ genus` deserializes JSON objects into typed records. Missing
# defaultable fields zero-initialize; missing mandatory fields fail at runtime
# (recoverable via `вҮҘ`). Extra JSON keys are ignored.

genus Persona {
    textus nomen
    numerus aetas sponte
    textus regio = "Roma"
    instans born
}

incipit {
    fixum valor payload вҶҗ {
        "nomen": "Marcus",
        "born": "1979-05-27T07:32:00Z",
        "extra": "ignored"
    } вҶҰ valor
    fixum Persona good вҶҗ payload вҶҰ Persona
    adfirma good.nomen вүЎ "Marcus"
    adfirma good.aetas вүЎ nihil
    adfirma good.regio вүЎ "Roma"

    fixum valor stale вҶҗ { "nomen": "Livia" } вҶҰ valor
    fixum Persona recovered вҶҗ stale вҶҰ Persona вҮҘ good
    adfirma recovered.nomen вүЎ "Marcus"

    fixum valor boxed вҶҗ recovered вҶҰ valor
    fixum Persona roundtrip вҶҗ boxed вҶҰ Persona
    adfirma roundtrip.nomen вүЎ "Marcus"
    adfirma roundtrip.regio вүЎ "Roma"

    nota roundtrip.nomen
    nota roundtrip.regio
    nota roundtrip.born вҶҰ textus
}

Expected output:

Marcus
Roma
1979-05-27T07:32:00Z

examples/corpus/conversio/valor-scalaria.fab (canonical В· conversio)

Scalar valor вҶҰ T extraction via FromValor (variant match, fractus widen, textus|instans wire).

# =============================================================================
# valor вҖ” Scalar valor вҶҰ T extraction via FromValor (variant match, fractus widen, textus|instans wire).
# =============================================================================
#
# What this teaches:
#   вҖў Scalar extraction вҖ” `valor вҶҰ T` extracts typed values from dynamic JSON carriers using variant matching.
#   вҖў Widening вҖ” `valor вҶҰ fractus` widens `Numerus` variants losslessly.
#   вҖў Wire text вҖ” `valor вҶҰ textus` accepts both `Textus` and `Instans` wire strings.
#   вҖў Recovery вҖ” `вҮҘ` provides a fallback on variant mismatch.
#
# Common mistakes:
#   вҖў Assuming `valor вҶҰ textus` works for non-text valor variants вҖ” only Textus and Instans wire strings are accepted; other variants fail without `вҮҘ` recovery.
#
# See also: вҶҰ, вҮҘ, instans, textus
# =============================================================================

# conversio вҖ” scalar valor extraction
#
# WHY: `valor вҶҰ T` is the default runtime extraction for dynamic JSON carriers.
# Scalar arms match `Valor` variants; `valor вҶҰ fractus` widens `Numerus` losslessly;
# `valor вҶҰ textus` accepts `Textus` and `Instans` wire strings. Typed datetime
# provenance stays on `valor вҶҰ instans` (see conversio/instans.fab). `вҮҘ`
# recovers on variant mismatch.

incipit {
    fixum valor asInt вҶҗ 42
    fixum numerus count вҶҗ asInt вҶҰ numerus
    adfirma count вүЎ 42

    fixum valor asFloat вҶҗ 3.5
    fixum fractus ratio вҶҗ asFloat вҶҰ fractus
    adfirma ratio вүЎ 3.5

    fixum valor intForFloat вҶҗ 7
    fixum fractus widened вҶҗ intForFloat вҶҰ fractus
    adfirma widened вүЎ 7.0

    fixum valor asBool вҶҗ verum
    fixum bivalens flag вҶҗ asBool вҶҰ bivalens
    adfirma flag вүЎ verum

    fixum valor asText вҶҗ "salve"
    fixum textus greeting вҶҗ asText вҶҰ textus
    adfirma greeting вүЎ "salve"

    fixum valor asWire вҶҗ "1979-05-27T07:32:00Z"
    fixum textus wire вҶҗ asWire вҶҰ textus
    adfirma wire вүЎ "1979-05-27T07:32:00Z"

    fixum valor asAscii вҶҗ 'yes'
    fixum ascii token вҶҗ asAscii вҶҰ ascii
    adfirma token вүЎ 'yes'

    fixum valor bad вҶҗ "not-a-number"
    fixum numerus recovered вҶҗ bad вҶҰ numerus вҮҘ 0
    adfirma recovered вүЎ 0

    nota count
    nota ratio
    nota widened
    nota flag
    nota greeting
    nota wire
    nota token
    nota recovered
}

Expected output:

42
3.5
7.0
verum
salve
1979-05-27T07:32:00Z
yes
0

examples/corpus/conversio/valor-tensor.fab (canonical В· conversio)

Compose valor вҶ” tensor roundtrip via lista bridges (no dedicated valorвҶ”tensor arms).

# =============================================================================
# valor вҖ” Compose valor вҶ” tensor roundtrip via lista bridges (no dedicated valorвҶ”tensor arms).
# =============================================================================
#
# What this teaches:
#   вҖў Indirect tensor conversion вҖ” `valor вҶ” tensor` has no dedicated codegen arms; it materializes through `lista<T>` as an intermediate step.
#   вҖў Roundtrip path вҖ” valor вҶ’ lista вҶ’ tensor вҶ’ lista вҶ’ valor, showing composition of existing operators.
#
# Common mistakes:
#   вҖў Expecting `valor вҶҰ tensor` to work directly without the lista bridge вҖ” there are no dedicated valorвҶ”tensor arms; compose through `lista<T>` instead.
#
# See also: вҶҰ, valor, lista, tensor
# =============================================================================

# conversio вҖ” valor вҶ” tensor compose roundtrip
#
# WHY: `valor вҶ” tensor` has no dedicated codegen arms. Extraction materializes
# `lista<T>` first; construction uses shipped `lista вҶҰ tensor` (`structa`) and
# flatten via `tensor вҶҰ lista` (`.planata()`), then `lista вҶҰ valor` boxing.

incipit {
    fixum valor arr вҶҗ [1, 2, 3, 4]
    fixum lista<numerus> xs вҶҗ arr вҶҰ lista<numerus>
    adfirma xs.longitudo() вүЎ 4

    fixum tensor<numerus, [4]> grid вҶҗ xs вҶҰ tensor<numerus, [4]>
    fixum lista<numerus> flat вҶҗ grid вҶҰ lista<numerus>
    adfirma flat.longitudo() вүЎ 4

    fixum valor roundtrip вҶҗ flat вҶҰ valor
    nota roundtrip

    fixum valor obj вҶҗ { "alpha": 10, "beta": 20 } вҶҰ valor
    fixum tabula<textus, numerus> scores вҶҗ obj вҶҰ tabula<textus, numerus>
    nota scores.accipe("alpha") vel 0
}

Expected output:

[1, 2, 3, 4]
10

examples/corpus/destructura/literal.fab (canonical В· existing-home)

JSON-shaped dynamic value literal for open object-shaped data.

# =============================================================================
# valor вҖ” JSON-shaped dynamic value literal for open object-shaped data.
# =============================================================================
#
# What this teaches:
#   вҖў JSON valor literals вҖ” `{ "key": value, ... }` creates dynamic valor objects using JSON5 syntax with trailing commas.
#   вҖў Nested objects вҖ” JSON valor literals can be nested for complex data shapes.
#   вҖў Constraint вҖ” JSON valor literals accept only constant values (strings, numbers, booleans, null, nested objects/arrays); use genus or tabula for variable-backed construction.
#
# Common mistakes:
#   вҖў Using retired bare object literals `{ key = expr }` instead of genus construction `Type { field = expr }` вҖ” bare `{ }` is now a JSON valor literal with quoted keys and `:` separator.
#
# See also: genus, вҲ·, ignotum, tabula
# =============================================================================

# destructura вҖ” valor literal expressions (inline JSON)
#
# { "clavis": expr, ... }              -- JSON object literal (valor)
# { "clavis": expr, "nidum": { ... } } -- nested JSON objects
#
# Anonymous Faber object literals (`{ key = expr }`) are retired. Bare `{ }`
# now denotes a JSON valor literal: keys are quoted JSON strings separated by
# `:`, values are JSON scalars, arrays, or nested objects. Trailing commas are
# permitted (JSON5-style). Duplicate keys are an error (second occurrence).
#
# GRAMMAR:
#   jsonLiteral :вҶҗ '{' (jsonMember (',' jsonMember)* ','?)? '}'
#   jsonMember  :вҶҗ string ':' jsonValue
#
# EXPECTED OUTPUT:
#   Empty valor, point coords, keyed fields, nested records.
#
# BACKEND: Go emitter does not lower object `sparge` into maps (whitelist:
# destructura/literal.fab).

incipit {
    # --- Empty and simple records ---

    fixum _ vacuum вҶҗ {}
    nota vacuum

    fixum _ punctum вҶҗ { "x": 10, "y": 20 }
    nota punctum

    # Text keys and constant values
    fixum _ forma вҶҗ { "clavis": 42 }
    nota forma

    # NOTE: JSON valor literals accept only constant values (strings, numbers,
    # booleans, null, nested objects/arrays). For variable-backed field
    # construction, use genus `Type { field = expr }` or tabula insertion.

    # --- Nested valor ---

    fixum _ nidum вҶҗ { "extra": { "medium": 1 } }
    nota nidum
}
жқҘжәҗ
  • ["examples/corpus/conversio/valor-boxing.fab"]
